title: Cross-Agent Credential Creation Ownership Verification
description:
The credential creation endpoint (api/v1/credentials.py) accepts an arbitrary agent_id in the request body and creates a credential for that agent without verifying that the caller owns or has authority over the target agent. Any authenticated agent can mint API keys for any other agent in the system, including agents in unrelated trust hierarchies. This was confirmed during MCP smoke testing where test-bot (069b3c54) successfully created a credential for postman-test-agent (069b2950) despite having no parent-child or delegation relationship.

Add authorization checks to the credential creation endpoint:
1. An agent can always create credentials for itself (agent_id == caller.id).
2. A parent agent can create credentials for its direct children.
3. A root agent can create credentials for any agent in its subtree (descendants only).
4. No agent can create credentials for agents outside its subtree, regardless of trust level.
5. Delegation relationships alone do NOT grant credential creation rights — only the trust hierarchy (parent-child) applies.

The same ownership verification must also be applied to credential rotation (rotate_credential) and credential revocation (revoke_credential) endpoints.
validation: Credential creation rejects requests where caller has no authority over target agent_id
Agent can create credentials for itself
Parent agent can create credentials for direct child agents
Root agent can create credentials for any descendant in its subtree
Agent cannot create credentials for agents outside its subtree
Delegation relationship alone does not grant credential creation rights
Same ownership checks enforced on credential rotation endpoint
Same ownership checks enforced on credential revocation endpoint
Returns 403 Forbidden with RFC 7807 problem detail when authorization fails
Audit log records denied credential creation attempts with reason
Tests verify all authorization scenarios (self, child, subtree, cross-agent denial)
Tests verify delegation-only relationship is insufficient for credential creation
